commands.review_summary:
This PR changes overview row menu-item actions so rows with detail submenus stay on the passive submenu action path and adds a z.ai regression test.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was run in this read-only review. The linked issue screenshot and current source make the bug source-reproducible because overview rows can have both a detail submenu and a provider-selection action.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor proof and maintainer review for the UX/accessibility tradeoff; there is no narrow automation repair to run without choosing the intended selection contract.

target.title: Fix z.ai overview submenu recursion
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep overview rows with detail submenus on the passive submenu action path.
- Add z.ai regression coverage that ensures submenu activation does not switch into provider detail.

## Why

Hovering the z.ai overview row could open a nested full menu because the row had both a detail submenu and a provider-selection action. Rows with submenus should let hover open only the detail submenu; plain rows still keep the selection action.

Fixes #1246.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `.build/lint-tools/bin/swiftformat Sources Tests --lint`
- `swift test --filter StatusMenuOverviewSubmenuTests` was blocked locally before project code compiled: the Command Line Tools install cannot load `PreviewsMacros` for the `KeyboardShortcuts` dependency `#Preview` declarations.
- `make check` ran SwiftFormat successfully, then was blocked when portable SwiftLint trapped loading `sourcekitdInProc.framework` under the local Command Line Tools install.
target.author: rajvardhanpatil07
